food_name: Red Unpolished Rice (Thai Red Cargo, Cooked)

local_names.thai: ข้าวแดง (หุงสุก)

aliases: Red Unpolished Rice (Thai Red Cargo, Cooked)
ข้าวแดง (หุงสุก)

english: Red Unpolished Rice (Thai Red Cargo, Cooked)
thai: ข้าวแดง (หุงสุก)

region_of_origin: South and Southeast Asia (Thailand; widely consumed in Singapore)
food_subgroup: Whole grain cooked rice — red unpolished (red cargo). 1 standard bowl = 180g cooked. GI ~55–60. Red bran pigmentation from anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins.

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn URI exists for Thai red cargo rice. Closest parent concept is unpolished brown rice (whole grain cooked rice). FoodOn URI left null pending a more specific term.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A003K is the closest FoodEx2 code available for red cooked rice; no exact code for Thai red cargo variety specifically.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for red unpolished rice. Left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of cooked red unpolished rice weighs approximately 180g, equivalent to roughly 220 kcal, and is the typical single-meal serving used in Singapore dietary guidelines.

nutrient_source.title: Red Rice — FAO/INFOODS South-East Asian Food Composition Table and USDA SR Legacy (brown rice as reference proxy)
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS South-East Asian Food Composition Table; USDA SR Legacy via USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Red rice (Thai red cargo) nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA food composition data for red rice and cross-referenced against USDA SR Legacy brown rice (USDA FDC ID 169704) as a reference proxy. Red rice and brown rice share closely comparable macronutrient profiles; the primary distinction is the anthocyanin/proanthocyanidin pigment content of the red bran. Values represent cooked state per 100g.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Thai Red Cargo Rice — University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI range of ~55–60 for Thai red cargo rice sourced from University of Sydney GI Database entries for red rice varieties. Midpoint value of 57 used. GL calculated as (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (57 × 46.1g per 180g serving) / 100 ≈ 26.3. Available carbohydrate per 180g = 25.6 × 1.8 = 46.08g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (3mg/100g; ~5mg per 180g bowl) with beneficial magnesium (38mg/100g; ~68mg per bowl) and dietary fibre
hypertension.guidance: Red unpolished rice is suitable for hypertension management due to its very low sodium content (~5mg per bowl), meaningful magnesium contribution (~68mg per bowl, approximately 17% of RDA), and dietary fibre; its profile is clinically equivalent to brown rice for blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~57) and moderate GL (~26 per bowl); anthocyanins may provide additional α-glucosidase inhibition beyond fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Red unpolished rice has a GI of approximately 57 and a GL of ~26 per 180g bowl — significantly lower than white rice (GI ~72, GL ~38–40) — and is clinically interchangeable with brown rice as a white rice substitute in T2DM dietary management; the anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins in the red bran may provide modest additional benefit via α-glucosidase inhibition.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero trans fat, meaningful dietary fibre, and anti-inflammatory anthocyanin pigments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Red unpolished rice is a heart-healthy whole grain choice with negligible saturated fat (0.1g/100g), zero trans fat, and dietary fibre (1.7g/100g; ~3g per bowl) that supports LDL reduction; the anthocyanin content provides additional anti-inflammatory benefit relevant to cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Anthocyanins inhibit NF-κB pathway and reduce TNF-α; magnesium has anti-inflammatory properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Red rice's anthocyanin pigments have demonstrated anti-inflammatory activity in cellular studies (NF-κB inhibition, TNF-α reduction), giving it a modest anti-inflammatory advantage over brown rice for rheumatoid arthritis dietary management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Red unpolished rice (Thai Red Cargo variety, cooked) is a whole grain rice with a distinctive reddish-brown bran layer that imparts a slightly nutty flavour and mildly chewy texture compared to white rice. The red colour comes from anthocyanins and proanthocyanidins concentrated in the outer bran. Per 100g cooked, it provides approximately 122 kcal, 2.5g protein, 0.4g fat, 25.6g carbohydrate, and 1.7g dietary fibre. A standard 180g bowl delivers roughly 220 kcal, 4.5g protein, and 3g fibre. Its glycemic index is approximately 57 (medium GI), substantially lower than white rice (GI ~72), with a glycemic load of ~26 per bowl. The anthocyanins may provide modest additional GI reduction via α-glucosidase inhibition beyond the fibre effect alone. For Type 2 Diabetes management, red rice and brown rice are clinically interchangeable as white rice substitutes. Red rice is widely available in Singapore at NTUC FairPrice, Cold Storage, and health food stores; HPB MyHealthyPlate recommends it alongside brown rice as a healthier rice option. It is naturally gluten-free, vegan, and halal.
common_user_queries: is red rice good for diabetes

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy brown rice as a reference proxy; no single approved source provides a dedicated red rice cooked entry with full micronutrient panel. GI is from University of Sydney GI Database range for red rice varieties using midpoint estimation. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for cooked red unpolished rice not available in FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy as a distinct entry; brown rice proxy does not provide reliable MUFA/PUFA split for red rice specifically. water_g set to -1: moisture content for cooked red rice not reported in the adapted source combination used. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero for plain cooked whole grain rice per USDA SR Legacy. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — naturally zero; red rice is a plant food containing no cholesterol. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg are 0.0 — confirmed negligible or zero for plain cooked rice per USDA SR Legacy and FAO/INFOODS SEA. All core macros (protein_g 2.5, fat_g 0.4, carbohydrate_g 25.6) are non-zero and sourced from FAO/INFOODS SEA adapted values. GI midpoint 57 used from University of Sydney GI Database range of 55–60 for Thai red cargo rice. GL per 180g bowl = (57 × 46.08) / 100 ≈ 26.3.
